shelfmark: Bodl. MS heb. c 13/22 + L-G Bib.VI.38
description: Copy of an epistle from Saʿadya Gaon to the Jews of Fustat, on the occasion of his assumption of the Gaʾonate in 928. This copy dates to the 11th century (ca. 1028 CE, based on verso). Saʿadya stresses the importance of the Oral Law, mentions the names of his supporters in Baghdad and urges the Jews of Fustat to maintain close links with him and his Academy, viz., Sura. (Information from Gil, Kingdom, Vol. 2, p. 27.) NB: Gil incorrectly lists the shelfmark for the L-G fragment as L-G Bib.VI.52 instead of L-G Bib.VI.38.
description_he: העתק אגרת מסעדיה גאון ליהודי פוסטאט בעת קבלת הגאונות ב-928, על חשיבות התורה שבעל פה ועל קשרים עם ישיבתו
